entries.title: Governance single-point-of-failure: no branch protection, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS on a repo that ships executable code to every install (C20)
entries.action_hint: Enable branch protection on dev (require PR review + status checks) and add .github/CODEOWNERS covering packages/providers/ + .github/workflows/ + install scripts.
entries.how_to_fix: GitHub *Settings → Branches → Add branch protection rule*. For `dev`: require pull request reviews (1 approver minimum), require status checks (the existing `Test Suite` workflow), require branches to be up to date before merging, do not allow bypassing. Also add `.github/CODEOWNERS` with at minimum: `packages/providers/ @coleam00`, `.github/workflows/ @coleam00`, `scripts/install* @coleam00`.
entries.threat_model_context: Does NOT require xz-utils-level sophistication. Concrete paths: (1) credential phishing against coleam00 or Wirasm; (2) stale OAuth token reuse from a developer machine or old CI system; (3) compromised browser session cookie via malicious extension; (4) malicious IDE extension running as the maintainer; (5) sloppy review of an attacker-submitted PR — 5 of 6 flagged security-adjacent PRs were Wirasm-self-submitted-and-merged; (6) compromised self-hosted runner.
entries.what_this_means: When you install Archon, you are trusting the maintainer's account security as tightly as you'd trust your own laptop's password. There is no automated gate that would catch a bad push before it reaches your next install.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This finding is about the upstream process gap, not current code shipping something harmful. The install script sha256-verifies the binary; the Codex LPE (F1) requires local write access to your home directory.

entries.title: Codex vendor binary resolver has no hash pinning — local privilege escalation via writable vendor dir (#1245)
entries.action_hint: Don't run Codex workflows on shared hosts until PR #1250 merges. Single-user laptops: low risk.
entries.how_to_fix: Wait for PR #1250 to merge. Or manually verify `~/.archon/vendor/codex/` permissions — if running on a shared host, disable the Codex provider until the fix lands.
entries.threat_model_context: Attacker must already have some local write access — dev container sharing a home mount, misconfigured Docker volume, prior malware, admin user on shared workstation. Not a remote-unauth attack.
entries.what_this_means: On a multi-user machine, another user who can write to ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ can drop a rigged binary that runs as you on your next Codex workflow, with your full environment and credentials.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This is not a remote code execution vulnerability. Single-user personal laptops are low risk because nobody else has write access to your home directory.

entries.title: 8% formal review rate, no branch protection, no CODEOWNERS — governance process gaps persist
entries.action_hint: Practical reviewer density is higher than it looks (Wirasm is an active second pair of eyes), but the formal gates are all missing.
entries.how_to_fix: Enable branch protection on dev. Add CODEOWNERS. Consider enabling a dependabot.yml for automated CVE PR opening.
entries.threat_model_context: The 8% formal gate means 92% of merges could land without a recorded second-pair-of-eyes decision. Combined with no branch protection and no CODEOWNERS, a credential-compromised PR to a critical path (providers, install scripts, workflows) would not be blocked by any automated gate.

entries.title: Active security work pattern — recent PRs show real engineering care
entries.action_hint: No action needed. Positive signal: the team treats security PRs as first-class and merges them.

meta_table.label: PR #1217
meta_table.value: ✅ Merged — explicit CLAUDE_BIN_PATH allowlist replaces SDK auto-resolution

meta_table.label: exec.ts pattern
meta_table.value: ✅ Uses execFile (not shell exec) — avoids interpolation injection

entries.action: Shared-host Codex users: hold until PR #1250 merges (F1 LPE). Single-user installs: safe today — pin to a tagged release. Step 3: nudge maintainers to merge axios CVE patch and enable branch protection.

section_01.pill_row: 🚨 Hold if using Codex on shared hosts · ⚠ 1 consumer mitigation · 3 steps
section_01.summary: **Most urgent:** if you run Archon with the Codex provider on any machine another user can log into, wait for PR #1250 before trusting Codex runs (Step 1). For single-user laptops (Step 2) you're fine to install today via the pinned install script. Step 3 is the dependency patch to watch.

section_02.summary: 9 findings total in the full scan (4 kept in this subset). **The Critical finding (F0) is structural:** no branch protection on `dev`, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS — a single maintainer-credential compromise ships arbitrary code to every install on the next release. Start with F0 (Critical) and F1 (Codex LPE) if time-pressed.

split_axis_decision.reasoning: The governance gap (F0 Critical) applies to every audience equally. But the Codex LPE (F1 Warning) creates an additive risk only on multi-user/shared hosts. A solo-dev on a personal laptop faces Critical (F0) but not the LPE escalation path. A shared-host operator faces both, which changes the install recommendation from 'pin carefully' to 'hold until #1250 merges'.

entries.audience: Installing today on a personal machine
entries.headline: Install pinned to a tagged release, and know the trust chain terminates at the maintainer's credentials
entries.summary: Install scripts sha256-verify the binary against checksums.txt — but both come from the same GitHub Releases URL. No branch protection on `dev`, no rulesets, no CODEOWNERS (F0 Critical). A single compromised maintainer credential ships code to every install on the next release with no gate. If you install anyway, pin to a specific tag (not `main`/`dev`) and subscribe to repo releases.

entries.audience: Shared host / multi-tenant / using Codex provider
entries.headline: Do not run in production until PR #1250 merges
entries.summary: Two Critical surfaces stack here: F0 (no governance gate upstream) plus F1 (Codex vendor binary resolver has no hash/signature pinning — local attacker with write access to ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ gets arbitrary-code execution under your user). Also watch PR #1153 (axios CVE-2025-62718).

selections.reason: Falsification: if the Codex binary resolver added sha256 pinning (which PR #1250 proposes), F1 would close. This is the single most actionable open item — it has a specific fix in review.
Falsification: if branch protection API returned 200 or rulesets were non-empty, F0 would move from Critical to at most Warning. The governance gap is the root cause behind both verdict entries.
Falsification: if formal review rate were above 50% with branch protection enabled, F4 would close as a non-finding and the overall governance picture would improve substantially.

paths: credential phishing against coleam00 or Wirasm (both public profiles, findable targets)
stale OAuth token reuse from a developer machine or old CI system
compromised browser session cookie via a malicious browser extension
malicious IDE/editor extension running as the maintainer
sloppy review of an attacker-submitted PR
compromised self-hosted runner on a release pipeline
dev container sharing a home mount with another user
misconfigured Docker volume exposing ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ to other containers
prior malware that landed via another vector and planted a rigged binary
admin user on a shared workstation with access to user home dirs

entries.headline: If you use the Codex provider on a shared host, hold off until #1250 merges
entries.non_technical: Archon downloads a Codex CLI binary to ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ without checking its hash. On a multi-user machine, another user who can write to that folder can drop a rigged binary that runs as you. The fix is open as PR #1250. Single-user laptops are low risk.
entries.command:
ls -la ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ 2>/dev/null
gh pr view 1250 -R coleam00/Archon --json state,mergedAt

entries.headline: Install Archon on your own machine — the install script verifies checksums
entries.non_technical: Installing now is fine on a personal laptop. The install script sha256-verifies the binary before running it.

entries.headline: Ask maintainers to merge the axios CVE patch and enable branch protection
entries.non_technical: Merge PR #1153 (axios CVE fix), enable branch protection on dev, add dependabot.yml, add CODEOWNERS.

entries.label: CREATED
entries.note: Repo created by coleam00 (Cole Medin at Dynamous). Initial architecture: Bun + TypeScript monorepo with workspace packages.

entries.label: SECURITY.MD ADDED
entries.note: Repo adds SECURITY.md at root with real private disclosure policy (email + GHSA). The difference between caveman no-advisory culture and real vulnerability management.

entries.label: ENV LEAK FIXED
entries.note: PR #1169 merges — prevents target-repo .env files from leaking into Archon-spawned subprocesses. Real security refactor. Closes #1135.

entries.label: RAPID RELEASES
entries.note: v0.3.0 → v0.3.6 in 4 days. All tagged. Homebrew formula auto-updated per release. Docker images published with semver tags to GHCR.

entries.label: RESOLVER HARDENED
entries.note: PR #1217 merges — explicit CLAUDE_BIN_PATH allowlist replaces SDK auto-resolution. Includes positive and negative CI smoke tests.

entries.label: AXIOS CVE OPEN
entries.note: External contributor files PR #1153 for CVE-2025-62718. Still open at scan time — 3 days with no merge. No dependabot config to have auto-opened it.

entries.label: CODEX LPE FILED
entries.note: Reporter shaun0927 files #1245 (Codex vendor binary LPE) and #1246 (web-dist attestation). Fix for #1245 proposed as PR #1250 the same day. Both open at scan time.

phase_4_structured_llm.timeline_intro: 7 events from repo creation to scan day. **Peak concern 2026-04-13 → 2026-04-16** (axios CVE open 3 days, Codex LPE filed the scan day). The team shipped real security hardening in #1169 + #1217 during the same window, so the signal is actively-working not stuck.

per_file.notes: Resolves Codex CLI path and hands to SDK without hash verification. On shared hosts with writable vendor dir, arbitrary code execution as current user.
Tier 1 auto-loaded agent-rule file. Content is benign development conventions today; leverage is 1:every-contributor.

executable_file_inventory.lead: ⚠ 2 Warning · ℹ 2 Info · ✓ 4 OK — 8 executable surfaces cataloged (7 scripts + 1 Tier-1 auto-loaded rule file). 2 need attention: the Codex binary resolver (F1 target) and the release.yml homebrew-update job that commits to dev without a review gate.
executable_file_inventory.layer1_summary: **8 executable surfaces inventoried.** The 2 Warning items are maintainer-side: merge PR #1250 (Codex binary pinning) and optionally gate the Homebrew auto-commit path in release.yml. The 4 OK items (install.sh, install.ps1, Dockerfile, docker-entrypoint.sh) are clean and sha256-verified where applicable. CLAUDE.md is a Tier-1 auto-loaded agent rule file — content benign today, leverage is every contributor session.

layer2_entries.dangerous_calls: fileExists() — no sha256 or signature check on resolved binary path
layer2_entries.notes: Warning: resolves Codex CLI from env/config/~/.archon/vendor/codex/ and passes to SDK codexPathOverride without hash pinning. Fix open in PR #1250.

layer2_entries.dangerous_calls: git push via GITHUB_TOKEN direct to dev on tag-push
layer2_entries.network: GitHub Releases fetch + git push to origin/dev
layer2_entries.notes: Warning: update-homebrew job commits homebrew/archon.rb directly to dev without review gate. Low risk in isolation; compound risk with F0 (no branch protection).
OK — downloads versioned binary from tagged release, sha256-verifies before install. SKIP_CHECKSUM defaults to false.

layer2_entries.dangerous_calls: None — Set-StrictMode -Version Latest, same sha256 verification logic
layer2_entries.notes: OK — PS1 parity with install.sh. F16 Windows surface covered.
OK — multi-stage build, drops root to appuser via gosu, no untrusted tarball fetches.
OK — fixes volume perms, configures GH_TOKEN via credential helper (not ~/.gitconfig), execs as appuser.

layer2_entries.dangerous_calls: None — static markdown, development conventions only
layer2_entries.notes: Info — auto-loaded for every contributor session in this repo. 813 lines of TypeScript/Zod/Bun contributor conventions. Imperative-verb grep found no prompt injection patterns. Leverage: 1:every contributor. Content benign today.

layer2_entries.dangerous_calls: None — EXIT trap pattern for safe rollback
layer2_entries.notes: Info — CI-only build pipeline. Uses bash EXIT trap to restore bundled-build.ts after bun build --compile. Not end-user facing.

executable_file_inventory.closing_note: **Note on CLAUDE.md auto-load leverage.** A future PR swapping the benign development-convention content for attacker instructions would take effect on every contributor's next Claude Code session. Combined with the governance gaps in F0/F4 (no branch protection on dev, no CODEOWNERS, 8% formal review rate), a PR modifying CLAUDE.md could land without a gate. Current content is benign; the structural risk is the leverage ratio.

pr_sample_review.intro: **6 security-adjacent PRs reviewed.** All 6 touch security-critical files (providers, installer, CI workflows). None had a formal `reviewDecision`. All were merged by Wirasm (the lead contributor), so practical review happened without the `reviewDecision` field being set.

coleam00 created 2019-02-03, 51 public repos, 6,641 followers, Dynamous company
More than owner's 343 — a second active maintainer

Real private disclosure policy — cole@dynamous.ai or GitHub private advisory

Real matrix test (ubuntu + windows), Docker smoke test

phase_4_structured_llm.coverage_intro: **160/300 PRs sampled, 7/7 execs inspected, 3 gaps noted.** Only 160 of ~461 closed PRs reviewed (300-PR API-limit window; older PRs not inspected). All executable files inspected. Windows PS1 surface covered. Monorepo has 11 inner packages — only root + root-lock inspected as the primary surface.

rows.result: ✅ OK — 769 files — pinned to `3dedc22` at scan start
6 of the 300-keyword hits — the flagged 6 in §03 read in full
⚠ Metadata only — Dependabot API 403 (admin scope); full lockfile audit not performed
✅ 1 found — CLAUDE.md at root (Tier 1 auto-loaded). Not CI-amplified.
⚠ Install path verified, installed artifact NOT verified — trust chain terminates at GitHub Releases (see F2 issue #1246)
✅ install.ps1 inspected; CI test matrix covers windows-latest
ℹ Not queried — transitive surface covered by CVE watch (F3)
`3dedc22` — if your tree differs, re-run the scan

pr_target_usage.note: Zero `pull_request_target` usage across 4 workflows — rules out that class of attack

entries: Older PRs (pre-2026-03) not inspected — sampling window stops at 300 PRs deep.
Dependabot alerts API requires admin:repo_hook — token used here lacks it; alert state unknown beyond 'no config file'.
Monorepo has 11 inner packages with their own package.json — per-package dep surface not enumerated here; root + root-lock inspected as the primary surface.

groups.headline: Open security issues filed by an external researcher — actively being fixed (3 findings)
groups.one_line_summary: Codex LPE + web-dist attestation gap + axios CVE override — none merged yet, all tracked publicly

items.claim: Codex vendor binary resolver lacks hash/signature pinning (issue #1245). On multi-user hosts, another user who can write to ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ gets arbitrary code execution as you on the next Codex workflow. Fix open in PR #1250.

`archon serve` downloads web-dist tarball AND its sha256 checksums from the same GitHub Releases URL (issue #1246). A release-manager token compromise defeats the verification. Reporter asks for independent attestation / sigstore.
Axios CVE-2025-62718 (NO_PROXY bypass via hostname normalization) override pending in PR #1153, open since 2026-04-13 — 3 days before scan. Reaches via @slack/bolt and @slack/web-api.

groups.headline: Governance gaps — low formal review rate, no branch protection (4 findings)
groups.one_line_summary: 8% formal review, no branch protection on dev, no CODEOWNERS, CI bot commits direct to dev

items.claim: 13 of 160 merged PRs have formal reviewDecision set (8%). Any-review rate is 58% (93/160) — a second pair of eyes exists in practice but formal gates are absent.
No branch protection rule on `dev` (the default branch) or `main`. Direct pushes possible, no required status checks at the branch level. API returns 404 — authoritative.
No CODEOWNERS file anywhere in the repo — no path-scoped review gate on hooks, workflows, or install scripts.
`release.yml` runs update-homebrew.sh then commits the updated Homebrew formula directly to `dev` using GITHUB_TOKEN. Low risk alone; compound risk with no branch protection.

groups.headline: Strong positive signals — real disclosure, pinned distribution, established maintainers
groups.one_line_summary: SECURITY.md with private reporting, sha256-verified installs, 7-year-old owner, active second maintainer

items.claim: SECURITY.md at repo root with a real private reporting policy (cole@dynamous.ai or GitHub's private advisory). External reporter used it to file four well-scoped issues with evidence.

Install scripts verify sha256 checksums by default (SKIP_CHECKSUM=false). Homebrew formula pins to release tag + per-platform sha256. Docker images build via GHCR with semver tags. Opposite of the caveman curl-pipe-from-main pattern.

coleam00 (Cole Medin): 7-year GitHub account, 6,641 followers, Dynamous company. Wirasm (791 commits) is the de facto dev lead — owner-merges-contributor-work is healthier than solo-maintainer for review.
PR #1169 blocks .env leaks into subprocesses; PR #1217 replaces SDK binary resolution with an explicit allowlist; exec.ts uses execFile (not shell); Docker entrypoint drops root to appuser via gosu.

813-line CLAUDE.md is Tier-1 auto-loaded by Claude Code for every contributor session. Imperative-verb grep found only project-convention statements (TypeScript/Zod/Bun discipline) — no prompt injection patterns.

phase_4_structured_llm.evidence_intro: 6 command-backed claims. **Skip ahead to items marked ★ START HERE** — the Codex LPE issue body, the install-script byte-match against archon.diy, and the review-rate dual metric. Those three are the falsification criteria for the split verdict.

entries.claim: Codex vendor binary resolver has no sha256 pinning (F1 / issue #1245)
entries.result: security(providers): codex vendor binary resolver lacks hash/signature pinning — LPE via writable vendor dir
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — issue body explains the resolver flow and the attack pre-condition (writable ~/.archon/vendor/codex/). Fix open in PR #1250.

entries.claim: archon.diy/install serves the same content as repo scripts/install.sh
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — archon.diy acts as a CDN/redirect for the repo's install script, not a separately-controlled distribution endpoint.

entries.command:
gh pr list -R coleam00/Archon --state merged --limit 300 --json reviewDecision,reviews \
  | jq '{ total: length,
          formal_review: [.[] | select(.reviewDecision != null and .reviewDecision != "")] | length,
          any_review:    [.[] | select(.reviews != null and (.reviews | length) > 0)]    | length }'
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — 13/160 formal decisions = 8%; 93/160 any-review = 58%. The gap is the practical-vs-formal reviewer pattern Archon runs on.
Confirmed fact — PR #1153 open since 2026-04-13, scan date 2026-04-16.

entries.claim: No branch protection on dev or main (F0 / C20)
entries.command:
gh api "repos/coleam00/Archon/branches/dev/protection"
gh api "repos/coleam00/Archon/branches/main/protection"
gh api "repos/coleam00/Archon/rulesets"
for p in CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS docs/CODEOWNERS .gitlab/CODEOWNERS; do
  gh api "repos/coleam00/Archon/contents/$p" 2>&1 | head -1
done
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — 404 is authoritative (token has repo scope, permission-denied would be 403). Empty rulesets. CODEOWNERS absent in all 4 standard locations. C20 escalates to Critical: active code flow (8 releases in 30 days) + ships executable binaries to user machines.

entries.claim: SECURITY.md with real private disclosure policy
entries.result:
# Security Policy

## Reporting a Vulnerability

If you discover a security vulnerability in Archon, please report it responsibly.

**Do NOT open a public issue for security vulnerabilities.**

Instead, email **cole@dynamous.ai** or use [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/coleam00/Archon/security/advisories/new).
entries.classification: Confirmed fact — real private channel + GHSA link. Confirms the disclosure culture is real, not performative.

catalog_metadata.short_description: Remote agentic coding platform — a harness that lets you drive Claude Code and Codex through Slack, Telegram, GitHub, or a web UI, with its own DAG workflow engine. Ships as a Bun-compiled CLI binary plus a server + web UI.
catalog_metadata.target_user: Developers who want to run Claude Code or Codex remotely from Slack/Telegram/GitHub/web
catalog_metadata.prior_scan_note: None — first scan; rename this file on re-run with date suffix.

editorial_caption.text: An 18,000-star remote agentic coding platform with a serious SECURITY.md discipline, pinned-and-checksummed distribution, and three open security issues that its maintainer is actively fixing in public — and no branch protection or CODEOWNERS on the branch that ships executable binaries to every install.

entries.what_we_observed: Classic branch protection on dev and main returns 404. Rulesets: []. CODEOWNERS absent in all four standard locations (API + local tarball). 8 releases shipped in the last 30 days. CLI binaries distributed via Homebrew, archon.diy/install, and GHCR.
entries.what_this_means: When you install Archon, you are trusting the maintainer's account security as tightly as you'd trust your own laptop's password. There is no automated gate that would catch a compromised-credential push before it reaches your next install. On an 18k-star repo that ships Bun-compiled binaries, the blast radius of a single credential compromise is every current and future user.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This finding is about the upstream process gap, not current code shipping something harmful. The install script sha256-verifies the binary against checksums.txt. The Codex LPE (F1) requires local write access to your home directory — it is not a remote code execution vulnerability.

entries.what_we_observed: packages/providers/src/codex/binary-resolver.ts resolves the Codex CLI binary from env var, config, and ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ and passes it to the SDK's codexPathOverride. The only gate is fileExists(). No sha256, no signature, no permission check on the directory. Fix open in PR #1250.
entries.what_this_means: On a shared workstation or dev container, another user who can write to ~/.archon/vendor/codex/ can drop a rigged binary. Your next Codex workflow runs it with your full environment — your API keys, your file access, your user credentials.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: This is not a remote code execution vulnerability. Single-user personal laptops are low risk because nobody else has write access to your home directory. The 99% case (personal development machine) is not affected.

entries.what_we_observed: 13 of 160 merged PRs in the last 300-window have reviewDecision set (8% formal). The any-review rate is 58% (93/160) — a human comment or approval happened on more than half of merges. Branch protection API returns 404 on dev and main. No CODEOWNERS file anywhere.
entries.what_this_means: The formal gate is missing: 92% of merges land without a recorded second-pair-of-eyes decision. Practically, Wirasm (791 commits) and coleam00 (343 commits) form a functioning owner+lead pair, but this arrangement is not encoded in GitHub settings.
entries.what_this_does_not_mean: It does not mean the repo is unreviewed. The 58% any-review rate and the active security PRs (#1169, #1217) show the team is engaged with security as a development practice. The gap is process formalization, not intent.

scan_sign_off.notes: Subset fixture for Phase 7 HTML renderer stress-test. Not a complete scan artifact — trimmed to 4 findings (F0 Critical, F1 Warning, F4 Warning, F7 OK) and 6 evidence entries. Full golden at docs/GitHub-Scanner-Archon.md.
